audit_items.proof_trace: The property holds IF BPO forks are correctly tracked in ForkContext.next_fork() separate from phase transitions. Lines 655 and 661-664 correctly keep next_fork_version=current and set next_fork_epoch from fork_context. However, ForkContext is external (eth2_libp2p) and its implementation cannot be verified—if BPO forks aren't properly registered there, next_fork_epoch would default to FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH instead of the actual BPO epoch.
audit_items.attack_scenario: If ForkContext fails to track BPO forks separately, nodes would advertise next_fork_epoch=FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH in their ENR, causing peer discovery issues and potential network splits when BPO forks activate.
